AircraftComposition.doc: Top-level composition containing all subsystem instances and signal routing.
AircraftComposition.name: AircraftComposition

part_def.doc: Leading-edge strake and cropped-delta wing sized to F-16 Block 50 geometry with 9g capability. This model includes the control system
part_def.name: AdaptiveWingSystem

actuation_command.doc: Actuation commands, sent to environment to track and calculate new position
actuation_command.name: actuation_command

port_def.doc: Commanded surface deflections in degrees for co-simulation with aerodynamic models.
port_def.name: SurfaceActuationCommand

direction_command.doc: Mission-computer command bus input. Desired change in speed vector
direction_command.name: direction_command

port_def.doc: Aerodynamic state summary exported from wing solver.
port_def.name: LiftState

part_def.doc: F-16 autopilot, takes in sensor data and translates it into a equivalent hotas command.
part_def.name: AutopilotModule

autopilotCmd.doc: HOTAS-equivalent command stream for mission computer blending.
autopilotCmd.name: autopilotCmd

feedbackBus.doc: Mission-computer flight status back into autopilot.
feedbackBus.name: feedbackBus

missionStatus.doc: Waypoint progression for logging and verification.
missionStatus.name: missionStatus

part_def.doc: F-16 aluminum/composite fuselage sized for high-supersonic operations with dorsal spine for avionics.
part_def.name: CompositeAirframe

part_def.doc: F-16 HOTAS + side-stick interface for pilot inputs and trim switches.
part_def.name: ControlInterface

bridgeInput.doc: External pilot command provided by an interactive bridge/frontend adapter.
bridgeInput.name: bridgeInput

port_def.doc: HOTAS-style control payload shared across control surfaces and mission computer consumers.
port_def.name: PilotCommand

pilotCommand.doc: HOTAS-control bundle forwarded for mission computer sensor fusion.
pilotCommand.name: pilotCommand

part_def.doc: Tracking position and velocities of the aircraft and feeds the sensors
part_def.name: Environment

actuation_command.doc: Reads the actuation and thrust commands to calculate the position, rotation and velocities
actuation_command.name: actuation_command

part_def.doc: Adapter component for visual/frontend coupling to FlightGear using generic socket communication.
part_def.name: FlightGearBridge

flightStatus.doc: Flight status exported for overlays and diagnostics.
flightStatus.name: flightStatus

port_def.doc: Mission computer status feedback consumed by autonomy and analysis models.
port_def.name: FlightStatusPacket

missionStatus.doc: Mission/autopilot status exported for overlays and diagnostics.
missionStatus.name: missionStatus

port_def.doc: Waypoint-tracking status produced by the autopilot for logging and requirement evaluation.
port_def.name: MissionStatus

pilotCommand.doc: Pilot command returned from the interactive frontend into the vehicle control path.
pilotCommand.name: pilotCommand

stateOrientation.doc: Aircraft attitude from the simulated environment.
stateOrientation.name: stateOrientation

port_def.doc: Air vehicle orientation expressed as Euler angles in degrees.
port_def.name: OrientationEuler

statePosition.doc: Aircraft position from the simulated environment.
statePosition.name: statePosition

port_def.doc: Local navigation frame expressed in kilometers (x=North, y=East, z=Up).
port_def.name: PositionXYZ

part_def.doc: Internal + conformal fuel storage management with transfer pumps feeding engine feed tanks.
part_def.name: FuelSystem

fuelState.doc: Telemetry describing remaining fuel mass/normalized level.
fuelState.name: fuelState

fuel_consumption_rate.doc: Propulsion-requested fuel mass flow entering storage manager.
fuel_consumption_rate.name: fuel_consumption_rate

part_def.doc: Simulation sink for debugging and recording mission/autopilot telemetry.
part_def.name: InputOutput

part_def.doc: F-16C/D mission computer blending pilot HOTAS inputs with fly-by-wire control laws and stores management.
part_def.name: MissionComputer

autopilotInput.doc: Autopilot command bundle using same HOTAS signal structure.
autopilotInput.name: autopilotInput

direction_command.doc: Command how to alter the current velocity vector, pith, yaw, roll
direction_command.name: direction_command

engineThrottle.doc: Structured throttle/fuel enable command to propulsion.
engineThrottle.name: engineThrottle

fuelStatus.doc: Fuel telemetry used for command blending and power limiting.
fuelStatus.name: fuelStatus

port_def.doc: Remaining fuel mass and normalized level for availability tracking.
port_def.name: FuelLevelState

manualInput.doc: Manual HOTAS command bundle from cockpit controls.
manualInput.name: manualInput

part_def.doc: Pratt & Whitney F100 / GE F110 class augmented turbofan powering the F-16.
part_def.name: TurbofanPropulsion

port_def.doc: Mass flow request or telemetry for turbofan fuel usage (kg/s).
port_def.name: FuelConsumptionRate

throttleCmd.doc: Mission computer throttle + afterburner command input.
throttleCmd.name: throttleCmd

port_def.doc: Engine throttle and fuel-enable bit for turbofan co-simulation.
port_def.name: ThrottleCommand

thrustOut.doc: Propulsion thrust telemetry exported to airframe.
thrustOut.name: thrustOut

port_def.doc: Propulsion output shared with performance models.
port_def.name: ThrustState

requirements.text: Match F-16 Fighting Falcon geometry: ≈15 m length, 10 m wingspan, single-seat cockpit.
Provide Mach 2 dash capability with sustained 9 g maneuver envelope.
Integrate an F100/F110 class augmented turbofan with afterburner throttle control.
Carry at least 3,100 kg of internal fuel with 8% reserve margin for recovery.
Provide full HOTAS pilot controls, digital fly-by-wire channels, and autopilot hold modes.
Support multirole loadouts across nine hardpoints with mission computer stores management.
It should be an unstable aircraft, the control loop should compensate for this.
It should support loading a number of waypoints that it should fy to in order
The system should be packaged and simulated as a SSP (system structure and parameterization) artifact
All models should be packaged as FMUs

Location is described in a local x/y/z grid in kilometers (x=North, y=East, z=Up) Absolute orientation is described in euler angles, North = 360° / 000°, East = 090°, South = 180°, West = 270 changes in orientation are described in +/- x for euler angles, pitch, yaw, roll, based upon absolute orientation
The waypoints should be injected into the simulation by utilizing SSP parameter set

The recommended interactive solution keeps ssp4sim as the simulation master and connects the packaged aircraft SSP to FlightGear through a dedicated bridge/adapter component. FlightGear is used as the visual frontend and pilot I/O frontend. Generic socket communication is preferred for the first implementation because it keeps the exchanged signals explicit and easier to debug than a larger native protocol mapping. The first timing solution should use the existing ssp4sim realtime execution option. A future Python API doStep(t) method and external package synchronization support are planned, but should not be introduced in the initial integration.
